title: Hidden Lotus
author: Zenith Bot
content:
# Tips and Tricks 

> 1. **Use as many redeem codes as possible.**

Doing so will help you progress your account faster because of the goodies in the codes.

> 2. **Always have troops training, 2 buildings upgrading, and a tech being researched.**

Not doing so will hinder your account progress as these 3 things are imperative for gaining power and account progression.

> 3. **Do Holy Site Guardians.**

This helps your commanders level up faster—increasing your commander power faster. More commander power = easier to get Hidden Lotus skin.

> 4. **Make a Farm.**

This will not only help you with resources in the early game, but will also help support your account in the late game during kvk. The more farms the better.

> 5. **Save AP bottles for events.**

Do NOT open AP bottles unless there is an event like Lohar’s Trial, Strategic Reserve, King of the Tribes, Clarion Call, or Pre-kvk. Saving AP bottles for these events will help you place better in the events listed above, getting your account extra rewards.

> 6. **Only use universal speedups when necessary.**

Saving universal speedups will help you later on in your account; on day 7 if you need a bit more power to hit 1.5m and get Hidden Lotus or when an event that rewards you for using speedups comes along.

> 7.  **Do NOT buy healing speedups from the mysterious merchant.**

Save your resources/gems for other things rather than buying healing speedups. You should not need healing speedups until kvk 1, so there is no reason to buy them early-on.

> 8. **Save your gems after hitting VIP 6.**

Do not spend gems on VIP points after reaching VIP 6. After unlocking the second building queue there is nothing worth pushing VIP for until VIP 10—which can be unlocked later on.

title: Dhalruk’s Puzzle Box
author: Zenith Bot
content:
### Event Rules

1. Spend Free tries (total of 5 free tries) or Mechanisms during the event to open Puzzle Boxes. Each time you open a Puzzle Box, you gain the treasure it contains.

2. Each level contains a Rare Treasure. Open it to gain all of the remaining treasures on the map and move on to the next level. 

3. Each Puzzle Box opened adds to your Puzzle Progress, which begins at 0.5% (you also have a chance of getting a critical success multiplier). When your Puzzle Progress reaches 100%, you can get access to Dhalruk's Private Collection, where you can choose an Ornate Chest and gain extra treasure. This will reset your Puzzle Progress to 0. Every time you access Dhalruk's Private Collection, the prize pool has a chance to reset according to the probability displayed. When the event ends your Puzzle Progress will be retained and kept for the next instance of the event. 

4. There is a use limit for Mechanisms. Once you reach the limit, you cannot use any more Mechanisms to open Puzzle Boxes, but you can still use your free tries. 

5. At the end of each event unused free tries will be reset to zero and remaining Puzzle Pieces will be saved in your inventory for use in the next instance of this event. 

6. Your City Hall must be at least Level 8 to participate in this event.
 
7. You can use an Auto Option for this event. It will automatically open boxes.

8. Event is 2 days long.

# Rewards and Probability

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1514093555759448174_1_img.png)

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1514093555759448174_2_img.png)

**Event Analysis**  
**1. Expected Value of Material Chests in Gems:**  
• We are focusing solely on the **material chests: Legendary, Epic, Elite, and Advanced**.  
• We are using the **Legendary Material Chest as our base unit of value (2400 gems)**.  
• **Epic: 1/4** of a Legendary Chest (**0.25**)  
• **Elite: 1/16** of a Legendary Chest (**0.0625**)  
• **Advanced: 1/64** of a Legendary Chest (**0.015625**)  
• In each grid, you get **1 Legendary, 1 Epic, 3 Elite, and 3 Advanced chests**.  
• We add up their relative values:  
  **1 (Legendary) + 0.25 (Epic) + (3 × 0.0625) (Elite) + (3 × 0.015625) (Advanced) = 1.5**  
• This means that the **total material chests you get from each grid have a combined value equal to 1.5 times the value of a Legendary Material Chest**.  
• Therefore the **gem value is 1.5 × 2400 gems = 3600 gems**.  

**2. Fixed Tries to Get Legendary Material Chest:**  
• The **average number of tries** to find the Legendary Chest is **10.83 (1 / 0.09234)**.  
• However, you can't actually do 10.83 tries. You have to do **whole number tries**.  
• To ensure a realistic scenario, we use **11 tries** to represent **slightly worse than average luck**.  
• We then use these **11 tries as a fixed number** to calculate the cost.  

**3. Gem Efficiency (No Free Tries):**  
• **Total Gem Cost: 11 tries × 400 gems/try = 4400 gems**.  
• **Percentage of value gained: (3600 / 4400) × 100% = 81.82%** (approximately).

**4. Gem Efficiency (With 5 Free Tries):**  
• **Mechanisms Needed: 11 tries - 5 free tries = 6 tries**.  
• **Total Gem Cost: 6 tries × 400 gems/try = 2400 gems**.  
• **Percentage of value gained: (3600 / 2400) × 100% = 150%**.  

**Conclusion (Material Chests Only - 11 Tries):**  
• **No Free Tries:**  
 • **Fixed tries to get legendary chest: 11**  
 • **You get approximately 81.82% of the value** of the material chests back in gems spent.  
• **5 Free Tries:**  
 • **Fixed tries to get legendary chest: 11**  
 • **You get 150% of the value** of the material chests back in gems spent.  

**THIS IS NOT INCLUDING THE 100% BONUS BC THAT’S A DIFFERENT VALUE FOR EVERY PLAYER.**

title: Protect the Supplies
author: Zenith Bot
content:
**How to Play**

1. Once summoned, the caravan will begin traveling to a random village on the map. Barbarian Raiders will attack the caravan along the way. 
2. To win, you must keep the caravan alive until it reaches its destination. 

**Event Difficulty and Points** 

1. Before summoning a caravan, you can add special challenges to the event to increase its difficulty and rewards. 
2. You can control the difficulty of each challenge by adjusting its Star level. Drag the sliders on the main event page to adjust the star level and view a detailed description of each challenge. 
3. At the start of the event, the highest star level you can select will depend on your power. You can unlock a higher star level for a challenge by completing the challenge at the previous star level.
4. Your total star level is the sum of the star levels you have chosen for each challenge. The higher your total star level, the harder the event will be. 
5. You can attempt this event multiple times. Only your best result will be recorded. 

**Legend Mode**

1. When your kingdom enters the offseason before Season 2, you will be able to unlock the extra-difficult Legend Mode for this event.
2. To unlock Legend Mode, you must successfully escort a caravan with every challenge at max star level. 

**Rewards**
You can claim rewards from the Quests page by successfully escorting a caravan with a certain total star level. 

**Important Notes**
If you fail to escort the caravan, it will still cost you one of your attempts, and your action points will not be refunded.

**Rewards**

**Normal Raiders** - Once you hit them they stop moving

- Before the Raiders show up on the map, circles appear which you can already target it 
- Kill Enraged Raiders first since they are not stopping once you hit them

title: Lucky Stall
author: Zenith Bot
content:
**Starting the Event** 

1. You can wish for whichever theme you prefer during the event period. The themes are "Construction", "Research", and "Training". 
2. The first wish is free of charge. Subsequent wishes will cost 1 Lucky Coin each. 
3. Lucky Coins can be obtained from the Lucky Spin event. 

**How to Play**

1. Select a theme and make a wish to obtain a discount in resource costs for the theme (effective only during the event period). 
2. Once you've made a wish, you can tap CHANGE to use 1 Lucky Coin to make a new wish. 
3. After making a new wish, you can choose whether or not you want to replace your existing resource discount with your new one. 

**Important Notes**

1. All resource discounts only apply to Food, Wood, Stone and Gold. 

2. The resource discount for the Construction theme only applies to building upgrades, and does not apply to constructing new buildings or decorations. 

3. In each instance of this event, each resource has a limit on how much it can be discounted. Once you've reached that limit, the resource costs for that resource will no longer be discounted. 

4. If you cancel an upgrade/training, you will receive a refund of all your resources as per usual, but it will still count towards your discount cap for the event. 

5. All Lucky Coins will be lost at the end of the event, so make sure to use them beforehand.

**Quests**

title: Dark Fortress Raid
author: Zenith Bot
content:
**How to Play**

This event is divided into 3 stages: 

1. Stage 1: Alliance members can earn Raid Coins by defeating Barbarians on the map. These Raid Coins can be donated via the event page to increase your alliance's damage to the Dark Fortresses. 
2. Stage 2: Your alliance leader or a titled officer can select a time and location for your Raid. After summoning up to 3 Dark Fortresses, your alliance can start the Raid. 
3. Stage 3: Create rallied armies to attack the Dark Fortresses you summoned. 

**Rewards**

1. You can earn personal and alliance rewards from this event. Personal rewards are based on how much damage you personally dealt during the Raid, alliance rewards are based on how much damage your alliance dealt in total. 

2. The alliance member who deals the highest total damage in the Raid will be the MVP. Depending on how much damage the MVP deals, your alliance rewards can be multiplied by up to 10.

**Important Notes**

1. Each alliance can only launch 1 Raid per event, though during the Raid you can attack the same Dark Fortress multiple times. 
2. Once your alliance leader or a titled officer sets the Raid time, they can summon a Dark Fortress to the kingdom or Lost Kingdom map and cannot be relocated after they're summoned, and all other Dark Fortresses your alliance summons must be summoned to the same map. 
3. Each Dark Fortress can be attacked by up to 8 rallied armies at a time. 
4. You can only receive rewards from this event once per event. If you deal 0 damage during a Raid, you will not receive any personal or alliance rewards. You'll still be able to earn rewards, however, by taking part in this event with a different alliance. 
5. Your Raid time cannot be modified within 5 minutes of the start of the Raid. 
6. You cannot start a Raid in the last 60 minutes of the event. 
7. At the end of the event, unused Raid Coins will be converted into personal points. 
8. You can only attack Dark Fortresses summoned by your own alliance.

title: Silk Road
author: Zenith Bot
content:
**Starting the Event** 

1. From the event page, your alliance leader or officers can select a difficulty level and summon a caravan. 
2. Summoning a caravan costs Alliance Credits. 
3. There is a limit to how many caravans you can escort in each instance of this event. Successfully escorting a caravan will cost 1 escort chance. 
4. Your alliance leader or officers can choose to abandon an ongoing Caravan escort at any time. Your alliance will not lose an escort chance if it quits midway through. 
5. Each governor can only dispatch 1 troop to escort the caravan.

**How to Play**

1. Once summoned, the caravan will begin traveling to a random village on the map. Barbarian Raiders will attack the caravan along the way. 
2. You will also encounter Barbarian Chieftains, who will stop the caravan from moving. You must defeat the Chieftain to continue. 
3. Your escort will be successful once the caravan reaches its target village. 

**Event Difficulty and Points**

1. There are multiple difficulty levels for this event. To unlock a difficulty level, you must complete the previous difficulty level while fulfilling certain requirements. 
2. You can earn individual points by attacking barbarians. The more damage you deal, the more points you'll earn. 
3. You can also earn points over time by keeping your troop within a certain range of the caravan.

**Rankings**

1. This event includes a leaderboard. Your alliance's ranking will be affected by the difficulty it chose. 
2. For alliances that chose the same difficulty level, the rankings will be based on the final condition of their caravans. 
4. You need 50,000 points to claim all rewards

**Rewards**

1. The higher the difficulty you selected, the better your rewards will be for successfully escorting the caravan. 
2. Your rewards will also be affected by the condition of the caravan when it arrives at its destination. The less damage it has taken, the better your rewards will be. 
3. Rewards will be sent to all alliance members via mail at the end of the escort. Rank rewards will be sent at the end of the event.

title: Golden Kingdom
author: Zenith Bot
content:
## Overview

### Requirements

Your City Hall must be at least level 17 to participate.

### Starting the Event

1. Tap the GO button on the event page to configure your troops for the Golden Kingdom.

2. You can configure up to 5 troops.

3. Once confirmed, troop selection cannot be changed unless you restart the event.

### How to Play

1. Each floor of the Golden Kingdom starts covered in fog.

2. As you clear the fog, you will encounter various helpful and hindering elements, such as:

- Enemy troops
- Arrow towers
- Healer huts 
- Abandoned Chests
- Gold Nodes
- 

3. Each floor has enemy Guardians that you must defeat. To advance to the next floor, you need to defeat the Guardian Chief.

4. The final floor contains Covetous Invaders.
These enemies will try to escape once attacked. Your goal is to deal as much damage as possible before they flee.

5. Troop losses carry over between battles.

### Rewards
1. For every 4 floors you clear, you will receive 1 Golden Chest containing valuable rewards.
2. On the final floor, you will earn a bonus reward based on how much damage you can deal to the Covetous Invaders. The more damage, the better the reward. You can only claim this bonus reward once per event.

### Event Description 

Governors clear a path through the fog, revealing random events as they venture forward. When a tile is cleared of fog, the 4 adjacent tiles (above, below, left, right) become revealable. The fog surrounding a Guardian cannot not be cleared until the Guardian is defeated. Defeat the floor's Guardian Chief to open up a portal to the next floor. 1 Golden Chest will be awarded for every 4th floor cleared. 

### Event Difficulty 
The strength of enemy troops in the "Golden Kingdom" event will scale with the participating Governor's Power to ensure a suitable challenge.

### Troop Setup 
Governors will use their own troops and commanders in battle, taking up to 5 troops with them. Once troops are set, they (including equipment and talents of Commanders involved) cannot be changed unless restarting the event. Troops recruited from Reinforcement Camps can be used in battle as well. 

### Troop Strength 
At the end of each battle, any surviving troops of either side will remain. Battle results will not influence the participating Governor's troop strength outside of the event. 

### Boosts 
All buffs from equipment, individual technologies, commander talents, VIP bonuses, civilization bonuses, city buildings and special city themes will take effect in the "Golden Kingdom" event.

### Features 
1. My Troops - Shows all your marches and additional marches you got during the event.
2. Relics - It can hold up to 9 different relics 
3. Blessings - It shows all your blessings you got during the event.
4. Reset Button - Gives you the option to restart from the beginning or from 4, 8, 12 Floor.
5. Gold - You can get gold from killing Guardians:
• Guardian Minion - 20 Gold
• Guardian Elite - 50 Gold
• Guardian Chief- 50 Gold

• Abandoned Chests gives from 20 to 100 Gold
• Gold Node gives from 20 to 100 Gold
• Disk of Destiny gives 100/200/500 Gold

title: Esmerelda’s Prayer
author: Zenith Bot
content:
1. Spend Wishing Coins to spin the wheel and receive the prize indicated by the pointer. Prize amounts are limited and will not be replenished once depleted. 

2. Prizes are divided into 8 types of normal prizes and 3 types of special prizes. When all 3 of the special prizes have been won, you will automatically receive all of the remaining prizes on the wheel, and do not need to spin it again. 

3. The chances of winning a certain prize type may decrease due to reductions inthe amount remaining. Correspondingly, the chances of winning other prize types may increase as the amount of this prize decreases. 

4. Each time the wheel is spun, you must spend at least 1 Wishing Coin. For subsequent spins you may spend up to 3 Wishing Coins. For each extra Wishing Coin you spend, you will gain an extra pointer. Each pointer is separate from the other pointers. 

5. You will gain extra prizes after winning a certain amount of prizes from the wheel. 

6. Once the wheel has been cleared, leftover Wishing Coins can be exchanged for Golden Keys, Crystal Keys, or speedups. Wishing Coins you do not exchange will be converted into Fortune Chests.

**Source of Coins:**

- Free - **35**
- - Esmeralda's Collection - **20**
- - Arms Training - **8**
- - Triarch Trouble - **7**
- Gemming - **40**
- - 4,500 Gems (50% Discount) - **10**
- - 27,500 Gems - **30**
- Bundle - **60**
- - 385$ - **60**

**Total amount of coins: 135**

**Rewards**

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1514153265179263037_3_img.png)

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1514153265179263037_4_img.png)

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1514153265179263037_5_img.png)

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1514153265179263037_6_img.png)

**Event Spin Efficiency Analysis – Material Return vs. Gem Cost**

**Objective:**
To determine the average number of spins required to complete the event and evaluate the material returns for both free-to-play (F2P) and minimal spender approaches.

**Simulation Parameters:**
100,000  simulations were run using Python to model the material drop rates across different spin thresholds.
The analysis focuses on two key spin milestones: 75 spins (commonly achievable by F2P players) and 90 spins (achievable with minimal spending, via the first three tiers of the event bundle).

**Average Spins to Complete the Event**

Average: 90.56 spins
Median: 96 spins

**F2P Scenario – 75 Spins**
- Material Drops (on average):
- - Legendary: 2.73
- - Epic: 6.51
- - Elite: 9.71

**Additional Reward:**
- 60-spin chest: 6 Legendary materials
- Total Legendary Materials (avg): 10.96
- - Gem Cost: 31,500 gems

This provides a baseline return for players not purchasing bundles, yielding 10.96 ≈ 11 Legendary materials for 31.5K gems.

**Minimal Spender Scenario – 90 Spins**
- Material Drops (on average):
- - Legendary: 4.10
- - Epic: 8.88
- - Elite: 10.00

**Additional Reward:**
- 90-spin chest: 16 Legendary materials
- Total Legendary Materials (avg): 22.95

**_By purchasing the first three tiers of the bundle to extend to 90 spins, players more than double their total Legendary material return compared to the F2P route (22.95 vs. 10.96), making it significantly more efficient for moderate spenders._**

**Conclusion:**
- While the average number of spins to complete the event is around 91, F2P players can still receive decent returns at the 75-spin threshold.
- However, minimal spending (to reach 90 spins) offers a substantial increase in efficiency and total rewards, more than doubling the Legendary material output.

title: Esmerelda’s House
author: Zenith Bot
content:
1. The event is 2 days long.

2. Each player has 1 Free Chance per day ( 2 in total) per event.

3.  There are a total of 75 chances per day:
Spin of 5 Chances cost - 3,600 Gems
Total amount for 75 chances - 54,000 Gems

4. To reach max rewards from ,,Extra Rewards Section" players need to do 100 Chances
- 100 Chances - 72,000 gems.

**Rewards**

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1514153544620576830_1_img.png)

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1514153544620576830_2_img.png)

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1514153544620576830_3_img.png)

**Esmeralda’s House Analysis**

**First 72 Spins**
1 Free Spin → 0 gems
1 Spin at 400 gems → 400 gems
70 Spins using 5-spin option:
70 / 5 = 14 sets
14 × 3600 gems = 50,400 gems

**Total Cost for 72 Spins:**
50,400 + 400 = **50,800 gems**

**Additional Spins to Reach 100**
After 72 spins, reset free and 400-gem spin
1 Free Spin → 0 gems
1 Spin at 400 gems → 400 gems
26 spins remaining
Using 5-spin option (5 × 3600 gems) → 25 spins (5 sets) = 18,000 gems
1 single spin at 800 gems → 800 gems

**Total Cost for 72 to 100 Spins:**
18,000 + 800 + 400 = **19,200 gems**

**Total Gem Cost for 100 Spins**
50,800 + 19,200 = **70,000 gems**

---
**Legendary Material Choice Chests Earned**
**From Drop Rates (100 Spins)**
Each drop percentage is multiplied by 100 spins:
Legendary Material Choice Chest ×3 (1.234%) → 1.234 × 3 = 3.70 chests
Legendary Material Choice Chest ×1 (2.057%) → 2.057 chests
Epic Materials Choice Chest ×3 (4.115%) → 4.115 × 3 = 12.345 epic chests → 12.345 / 4 = 3.086 legendary equivalent
Epic Materials Choice Chest ×2 (8.23%) → 8.23 × 2 = 16.46 epic chests → 16.46 / 4 = 4.115 legendary equivalent
Epic Materials Choice Chest ×1 (12.345%) → 12.345 epic chests → 12.345 / 4 = 3.086 legendary equivalent
**Total from Drop Rates:**
3.70 + 2.057 + 3.086 + 4.115 + 3.086 = **16.044 Legendary Chests**

**From Bonus Rewards (100 Spins)**
Spin 10: 2 Epic Chest → 2 / 4 = 0.5 Legendary Chests
Spin 20: 3 Epic Chest → 3 / 4 = 0.75 Legendary Chests
Spin 40: 1 Legendary Chest + 3 Epic Chests → 1 + (3 / 4) = 1.75 Legendary Chests
Spin 70: 2 Legendary Chests
Spin 100: 3 Legendary Chests
**Total from Bonus Rewards:** 0.5 + 0.75 + 1.75 + 2 + 3 = **8 Legendary Chests**

---
**Total Legendary Material Choice Chests Earned**
16.044 + 8 = **24.044 Legendary Chests**

---
**Average Gem Cost per Legendary Chest**
70,000 gems / 24.044 = **2,911.33 gems per Legendary Material Choice Chest**

Since each Legendary Chest is valued at 2,400 gems, we now compare the efficiency:

2,911.33 / 2,400 = **1.213**

This means that the actual cost per chest is about **21.3%** higher than its stated value.

20% of this event at level 13,
55% of this event at level 20,
80% of this event at level 23.

On Day 7, you can still donate items to upgrade the event. But you can't obtain them via in-game activities. The only option left on Day 7 is to buy the Event Bundles.

### Grind Mode ###
1. 2888 xp to complete all 25 levels. 
2. Assuming one item provides 1.5xp, 1926 items are needed to finish upgrading the event. 
3. Assuming you finish minimum 80 side quests in the event for 160 items, leaving 1766.
4. Suppose 1 item provides 1.5 towards the event, you would need 1177 items to get the event to level 25, at 168 items/day for 7 days.
### Relaxed ###
When outside of KvK, collecting rss from your city every 10min is the best way to finish the event if you have the time to invest.

- Pop a KD production buff (+ 20%) 
- Use production items (+ 25%)
- 15% or 20% rss production rune
- Use small marches to gather 
- Chaining with natural AP

Also note this is assuming lvl 25 production buildings. I usually get a little over 300 event items per day just from city rss.


So pace yourself instead of rushing to finish on the Day 6.

title: Fisherman’s Fortune
author: Zenith Bot
content:
**How to Play**

1. Choose one of the available Fishing Spots to start fishing. 
2. Fishing Spots are shared between multiple kingdoms. Multiple governors can fish from the same spot at the same time. 
3. There are two ways to fish: angling and netting. These methods require different amounts of Bait.
4. Different catches have different rarities. Mussels cannot be caught directly. They will instead be awarded to the governor that catches the last fish at a Fishing Spot. 

**Rewards**

1. Catches can be exchanged for Sea Shillings in the Catch Bag. 
2. Sea Shillings can be exchanged for various rewards in the Fish Market. 
3. The goods offered at the Fish Market will vary depending on your kingdom's current Season.

**Fishing Spot Generation**

1. At the start of the event, a number of Fishing Spots will be generated based on your kingdom group's level of activity. As such, the number of Fishing Spots generated may not be the same for different kingdom groups. 
2. Every 8 hours, new Fishing Spots will be generated for your kingdom group based on how many fish are left in your current Fishing Spots. 
3. If there are less than 8 hours left in the event, no new Fishing Spots will be generated. 

**Important Notes**

At the end of the event, your unused Bait, Sea Shillings, and fish will NOT be lost, and can be used in the next Fisherman's Fortune event. Your fish will not be visible in your inventory.

title: Hunt for History (Hammer)
author: Zenith Bot
content:
# How this guide works:
**Based on Predetermined Selection**

- **Floor 1** - Use mainly. On average uses about 40-60 hammers. 

- **Floor 2** - If you get wrecked using Floor 1. Count average if you don't get Floor 1 around 10 - 12 switch to Floor 2

There are at least two or more other boards shuffled into this game mode, but this will give you some edge at least.

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1506388016732569680_0_img.png)

# **Archaeology / Hammer Event Analysis**  

# **Event Mechanics:**  
- **26 slots per floor**, **~13 slots** on average to find the main reward.  
- **5 floors needed** to reach a legendary BP frag (8 total per run).  
- **Hammers cost 200 gems each**.  
- **Side rewards exist but minor mats are negligible**.  

# **Gem Cost Breakdown:**  
- **Pure gem spend:**  
  - **5 × 13 × 200 = 13,000 gems** for **8 BP frags + 4 epic mats**.  
  - **Valuing epic mats at VIP price**, BP frags cost **~1325 gems each**.  
  - **Egg event cost per BP frag:** ~900 gems → **Egg event is better for pure gem spending**.  

# **Free Hammers Reduce Cost:**  
- **30 free hammers per event** drop the cost to **~7,000 gems**.  
- **New cost per BP frag: ~575 gems** (for the first legendary BP drop).  
- **With free hammers + gemming, this is better than the Egg event**.  
- **Max 30 free hammers per cycle, but hammers carry over between events**.  

# **F2P & Low-Spender Strategy:**  
- **Save hammers for 1 event, then use 60 hammers next cycle** and gem the rest.  
- **The more events you save up for, the more free BP frags you get.**  

# **For Bundle Buyers:**  
- **Hammers should be valued at ~148 gems each** for purchasing decisions.  

# **Additional Notes:**  
- **Epic mats can only be chosen as mid-floor rewards for up to 100 floors.**  
- **These are minimum cost estimates**—if you value epic BP frags more than epic mats, BP frag costs drop further.  

---

# **TL;DR:**  
- **Pure gemming? Not worth it (Egg event is better at 900 gems per BP frag).**  
- **Using free hammers + gemming? Hammer event wins (~575 gems per BP frag).**  
- **Best strategy:** Save hammers between events to maximize free BP frags.  
- **Buyers:** Value hammers at **148 gems each** before purchasing.

title: Commander Swap
author: Zenith Bot
content:
## General Info ## 
- City Hall must be level 8
- Event lasts 5 days
- Only two swaps are allowed during the event
- Commanders must be permanently recruited in order to swap

## Tickets ##
- Gems: 1 Ticket cost 100 Gems. 120 tickets can be purchased with gems for 12,000 Gems
- Bundles: Each $5 bundle contains 12 Tickets. Bundle can be bought 10 times to receive 120 Tickets for $50.

## Swap Ticket Costs ##
```5555 ↔︎ 1000 (2000, 3000, 4000) ⇒ 120 Tickets
5555 ↔︎ 1110 (2110, 3110, 4110) ⇒ 120 Tickets
5551 ↔︎ 1000 (2000, 3000, 4000) ⇒ 72 Tickets
5555 ↔︎ 2111 (3111, 4111, 5111) ⇒ 72 Tickets
5555 ↔︎ 5111 (5211, 5311, 5411) ⇒ 36 Tickets
5555 ↔︎ 5511 (5521, 5531, 5541) ⇒ 12 Tickets
5555 ↔︎ 5551 (5552, 5553, 5554) ⇒ 2 Tickets
5555 ↔︎ 5555 ⇒ 2 Tickets```
## Use the Preview option to count total needed tickets before you buy and spend on tickets. ##
If you upgrade a commander to 4 Stars as well as having upgraded skills, that will help you drop your tickets cost as seen above.

## Examples for Optimal Ticket Spending ##
Swapping for a maxed 5555 Commander by : 
1. Upgrade your 2 Commanders to 4 Stars 5111 
This will cost you 50gh + 36 each
Total of 100GH + 72 tickets = 100GH + 7,200 Gems

2. Use your 2 Commanders at 4 Stars 1111
This will cost you 72 each
You will need to max the tickets with gems + buy 2x $5 bundles
Total of 144 tickets = 12,000 Gems + 10$
Or use less gems and buy more bundles, it’s all up to you!

3. Mix of both top options, 1 Commander at 4 Stars 1111 
& 1 Commander at 4 Stars 5111
Total of 50GH + 108 Tickets = 50GH + 10,800 Gems

## Please Note ##

- You are not allowed to swap Minamoto, Hannibal, Æthealfæd, Seondeok, Cleopatra, Casmir, Pepin, or Ishida

title: Strategic Reserve
author: Zenith Bot
content:
## Strategic Reserves Guide
> Supply Boxes you can get in 3 ways:
> 1. Gathering RSS on the map
> 2. Collecting RSS in city 
> 3. Defeating Barbarians 


### Gathering RSS on the Map

> The day before this event comes send your gathering marches around 2-3 hours before reset (when the event starts). This way you will already get Supply Boxes at the very start of the event. 
> During the event players can send gathering marches to the higher nodes and pull them back every 3 -4 times (possible more) before or to go to the Zone 1 and gather RSS from the lower LvL of the RSS node. 
> Make sure your 24H Gathering Boost is always active. 
> When your KD do a Gathering Buff make sure you have spent your AP so it doesn't stay full while you are taking advantage of the buff. 

### Collecting RSS in the city

> Make sure your RSS Production items are always active.
> Every 15 minutes collect RSS from your city. 

### Defeating Barbarians

> If you are killing barbarians with 5 marches make sure you use Peacekeeping Commanders as your primary commander to reduce the AP cost. 
> Another way, which is better but it does take a little more time is chaining. Preferably to chain with more marches at once.The same applies if you are at home or in the Lost Kingdom.
> There is one interesting way to get Supply Boxes from defeating barbarians without spending AP. However it requires a little bit of practice. 
> Teleport as close as possible to the Holy Site. 
> Take your chaining march and and start summoning low Lvl barbarians but you will aim to hit the Guardian this way you will spend 0 AP and get Supply Boxes. You will need to make sure to drag a Guardian closer to your city as your barbarians will be summoned closer to your city. 
> Similar option with waiting reset as for gathering RSS on the map. Spend your AP on a day before the event comes so at the reset you can already start with chaining at the lower AP cost but make sure your AP bar is full till max and spend an extra 500 AP. 

**_Important Note:_** 
- Don't take an extra 500 AP and leave it aka go over your limit (Example: 2,300/1,800 AP bar) and not spend it. You are wasting your AP usage like that and it's not recovering.
- Don't farm while your AP is full. One more way of wasting your AP usage. Once you spend your AP, send your farmers and gather Supply Boxes while your AP is recovering. 

## Extra Buffs
1. Rune - priority Action Point Recovery Rune 
2. RSS Production and Gathering Boost Items (24H only)
3. City skin - AP Recovery or RSS Production
4. Queen Title - Gathering Speed

title: Mercantile Melee
author: Zenith Bot
content:
**Event Phases**

2 Trading Periods:

- Day 1: Start → 23:59 UTC

- Day 2: 00:00 UTC → End


Each period: 2 Escorts + 2 Loots (unused chances don’t carry over).


**Escorting Caravans**

1. Only possible until the end of Day 1 of the 2nd trading period.
2. Max 2 caravans at a time.
3. Caravan rarity can be refreshed (1st free, then costs items).
4. Escort troops defend the caravan. If defeated, the caravan loses cargo.
5. Troop losses are restored after battle.


**Looting Caravans**

1. Max 2 loots per period.
2. Can loot caravans of non-allied (or non-coalition in SoC) governors.
3. Victory grants a random share of cargo.
4. Troop losses are restored after battle.


**Important Notes**

1. Rewards scale with City Hall level + caravan rarity.
2. Caravans have 2 Durability, lose 1 when looted; at 0, can’t be looted.
3. If you leave Lost Kingdom mid-escort, you lose the caravan & rewards.
4. Higher City Hall = more escort/looter troops (max 3).

**Rarity Odds**

Caravan rarity odds are as follows: 

- 1st Refresh: Common (32%) / Advanced (26%) / Elite (21%) / Epic (16%) / Legendary (5%) 

- 2nd Refresh: Common (30%) / Advanced (25%) / Elite (20%) / Epic (15%) / Legendary (10%)

- 5th Refresh: Common (25%) / Advanced (21%) / Elite (17%) / Epic (12%) / Legendary (25%)

title: Realm of Mystique
author: Zenith Bot
content:
**Group Composition:**  
- You need **3 DPS** and **2 Healers**.  
- Healers should **always** prioritize healing.  

**DPS Strategy:**  
- Focus on **single-target attacks** with **Meteor** for maximum damage.  

**Healer Strategy:**  
- Prioritize **AoE healing**—over time, this will also deal damage.  

### **First Boss Strategy:**  
- **Healers:** Keep **AoE healing active at all times** to sustain the team.  
- **DPS:** Focus on **single-target attacks with Meteor** for maximum damage.  

### **Second Boss Strategy:**  
- **Kite the boss** around the arena.  
- It spawns a **totem as its active skill**, which heals it.  
- If the totem isn’t dealt with, the fight will drag on indefinitely.  

### **Third Boss Strategy:**  
- Treat this fight like a **Ceroli boss**—focus on mechanics, positioning, and teamwork.  

### **Upgrades:**  
- Invest in **revive stones, skills, and energy generation** to sustain the fight.

title: Shadow Legion
author: Zenith Bot
content:
**About the Shadow Legion**

1. Shadow Legion troops have unique skills.
2. Peace Shields offer no protection.
3. If out of combat for 5+ minutes, troops gain speed—lost if attacked.
4. Elite troops appear every 5 waves.


**Requirements**

1. Must be in an alliance with 30+ members.
2. Only 1 troop per governor can be sent.
3. Leaders/officers start the event.
4. Each alliance can play once per event.


**Gameplay**

1. Dark Fortresses spawn on the map and launch 25 waves of attacks.
2. Each player is eliminated if breached twice.
Survive all waves to complete the event.
3. The event ends if all alliance members are eliminated.


**Difficulty & Points**

1. Difficulty chosen by leaders/officers before start.
2. New levels are unlocked by surviving with 15+ members.
3. Earn individual & alliance points for defeating enemies.


**Rewards**

1. Reach point milestones to unlock reward tiers.
2. Only the highest unlocked tier is awarded.
3. Rewards sent via in-game mail after the event.


**Important Notes**

1. Can only activate in one kingdom (original or Lost).
2. Teleporting counts as one breach.
3. Leaving your alliance resets your individual points.

title: Lucrene Scrolls
author: Zenith Bot
content:
Overview

- The event is 35 days long and consists of 50 levels in total. 
- To finish the event the player needs 5,000 points. Each level needs 100 points. 
- To complete an event, players need to do weekly and seasonal challenges. 
- Each of them contains different missions with a certain amount of points. 
- Unlike Crystal Quest players can buy levels with gems from day one. 
- One Level = **750**

**Weekly challenges** are spread into 5 weeks. Every week the new section of missions will be unlocked. Contains different sorts of missions as:

- Resource Gathering 
- Killing Guardians
- Training Troops
- Building Alliance Flags
- Healing Troops
- Increasing Power
- City Resource Collection
- Spending minutes

**Seasonal Challenges** contain 7 different missions. All 7 missions are open from the start till the end of the event. The following missions: 

**Reliable:**
- Log in for 25 days 
- Reward: 400 Points 
📒: It doesn't need to be 25 consecutive days

**A Well-Trained Army:**
- Train 270,000 units 
- Reward: 400 Points
📒: It counts any Tier Troop ( T1 - T5)

**Nature's Gift:**
- Get 154,050,000 resources 
- Reward: 400 Points
📒:  It counts collection from city & map gathering 

**Thorough:**
- Complete 510 daily objectives 
- Reward: 400 Points 
📒: Per day 23 daily objectives - 510 Daily Objectives can be done in 23 days

**Unyielding:**
- Destroy 230 barbarian forts
- Reward: 400 Points
📒: In LK 1 Barbarian Fort = 2 Forts

**Battlefield Native:**
- Defeat 954 barbarian troops  
- Reward: 400P Points 
📒: It counts Marauders. KvK Barbarians count double. 

- Weekly Challenges give in total **1,610 Points**
- Seasonal Challenges give in total **2,400 Points**
- Daily Chest gives in total **1,750 Points**
📒: Players get 50 points when they reach 50 points in their Daily Activity Missions.
**Total amount of points: 5,760**

title: Melon Market
author: Zenith Bot
content:
Gather resources and complete daily quests to obtain Egrimelons. Sell your Egrimelons or steal from allies to earn Melon Money. 

**To get Egrimelons**
- Complete daily quests
- Melon Masters will send you 10 per hour. 
- You can claim 120 from the Melon Masters a day. 
- You can steal other governors melons, but cannot see the amount you're stealing. (max 80 per acc, for 10 accounts each day)
- You can only steal 10 times a day, once per governor account.

**Daily quests**
- Defeat 50 barbs - 10 melons
- Buy 10 items from Courier - 20 melons
- Gather 5m rss from map - 10 melons
- Train 4K troops - 20 melons 

**Selling Melons**
- Prices vary from stall to stall. (200-600 Melon Money) 
- You can sell at a friend's stall for a better price. However, 5% of the Melon Money will go to the stall owner as a market fee. Stall owners  can get up to 30k Melon Money per day from fees. 
- Friends can sell up to 1500 Egrimelons at your stall each day. 
- Prices refresh at 00:00 UTC.

**Rewards**
- Use Melon Money at the Melon Mall for rewards. More spent gets you access to levels 2 & 3 for greater rewards. 

**Helpful Hints**
- 240 melons will be delivered a day, but you can only claim 120. You will stop receiving Egrimelons if you have over 80 unclaimed. 
- Work with friends/farms to steal greatest amount of Egrimelons possible per account  (80). You can only steal 10 times a day, 1 time per account. 
- If done correctly, you can steal up to 800 per day. 
- Any unsold Egrimelons at 00:00utc will be automatically sold at the previous days price.
- Use farms to sell to mains to get 30k a day.

**Summary**
You can get 360 melons from daily tasks and 720 from daily melon masters. That is 1090 total. If you only steal 80 melons a day for all 6 days, that gives an extra 480 melons for a total of 1570 melons to sell. Sell before reset and always aim for highest price possible. Sell immediately as shops can only sell 1500 a day.

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1506367186917392424_0_img.png)

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1506367186917392424_1_img.png)

![image](assets/guides_images/embed_1506367186917392424_2_img.png)

## Max amount of Melons per day ##
- Daily tasks 60 per day = 360 per event
- Idle income 120 per day = 720 per event
- Stealing max 80 per person = 4800 per event
- Market fee 30k per day = 1800 per event
- Bundle 50 per day = 50 per event
### Grand event total - 7730 ###


## Shop costs to max ##
### Level 1 ###
- 453,000
### Level 2 ###
- 454,000
### Level 3 ###
- 450,000

## Total cost to max shop - 1,357,000 ##

- To buy out shop, if you have max amount of 7,730 per event (1,289 per day), you would need to sell at a rate of 175 or more. 
- To buy out shop, if you have daily’s, idle income, stole 2400 (400 a day = 40 per steal), and bundle bought, that gives you 3530 per event (588 a day), you would need to sell at a rate of 385 or more.

## To max rss from shops on farms ##
- 630k is required to spend before level 3 will unlock. To buy the gold, that costs 80k. Total cost for farms to get rss is 710k
- With daily quests (360), idle income (720) and stealing 10 melons from 10 people (600), that's a total of 1680 melons for the event. 
- Selling 1680 for 422+ will get you the 710k needed to buy rss complete giving you around 1b worth of rss

title: Holy Knight’s Treasure (Egg)
author: Zenith Bot
content:
**Egg event equipment rotates in this order: **

- Helm / Pants
- Weapon / Accessory
- Chest / Gloves / Boots

> After chest/gloves/boots repeat back up to helm/pants

# **Egg Event / Holy Knight’s Treasure Analysis**  

# **Spin Costs:**  
- **1 spin** = 600 gems  
- **5 spins** = 2400 gems (480 gems per spin)  

# **Chest Rewards:**  
- **10 chests** – 5x Choice BP  
- **25 chests** – 10 Purple Choice + 4d Speedups  
- **45 chests** – 6 Choice BP  
- **70 chests** – 12 Purple Mats + some resources  
- **100 chests** – 9 Choice BP  

# **Drop Rates:**  
- **Purple Mats per spin:** ~0.47 (≈1000 gems per mat)  
- **BP Fragments per spin:** ~0.22 (≈2180 gems per BP frag)  

# **Gem Cost Breakdown:**  
- If valuing **Purple Mats at 600 gems each** (VIP Store price):  
  - **BP Fragments cost ~890 gems each** (cheaper than VIP Store’s 1500 per, which is also random).  
- **Best chest values**: 10 and 45 chests.  
- **At 100 spins, including chests**: **BP Fragments cost ~267 gems each**—way better value.  

# **TL;DR:**  
- **Purple Mats cost ~1000 gems each.**  
- **BP Fragments cost ~2.2k gems each.**  
- **Factoring both, BP Fragments cost ~900 gems, and Purple Mats ~600 gems each.**  
- **With chests, BP Frags drop to ~267 gems each.**

title: Gathering/Farm Accounts
author: Zenith Bot
content:
# 📌 𝐆𝐚𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐆𝐮𝐢𝐝𝐞:

> This guide will explain:

• How to collect resources efficiently
• Which commanders to level up
• What bonuses to use
• How to create and upgrade farms (additional accounts for gathering resources)

⸻

## ⚙️ 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠:

> The most effective troops for gathering resources are Siege units. In the early stages of the game, you should use only Tier 1 Siege, as they are inexpensive to heal if attacked while gathering.

Higher-tier siege units are generally unnecessary for routine gathering. The main difference between siege tiers is their load capacity, which is primarily useful for plundering cities or gathering at alliance resource fields.

To maximize efficiency, it is strongly recommended that you:
• Set timers for gathering trips, or
• Enable in-game notifications on your device,

so you are alerted as soon as your troops return and can immediately send them out again.

⸻

## 🎯 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬:

> Only commanders with the “Gathering” talent tree should be used and leveled for resource collection.

## 📈 𝐋𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐒𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐠𝐲:

> First Stage – Level 27
Upgrade your gathering commanders to Level 27 and invest talent points as shown in Screenshot 1.

Second Stage – Level 37
Once all your gathering commanders reach Level 27, continue upgrading them to Level 37, following the talent build shown in Screenshot 2.
This setup provides maximum gathering speed through talents.

⸻

## ⭐ 𝐑𝐞𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐝 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬:

> Elite Commanders:

• Sarka
• Gaius Marius

Both provide an 18% gathering speed bonus, making them excellent all-around gatherers.

Epic Commanders:

• Joan of Arc
• Wak Chanil Ajaw

These are the best Epic gathering commander, offering:

Joan of Arc:

• 25% gathering speed bonus
• 25% load bonus

WCA:

• 20% wood and stone gathering speed bonus.
• 30% food gathering speed bonus.

This makes them both ideal for efficiency and carrying capacity.

## 🌟 𝐋𝐞𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬:

> Choosing the best Legendary gathering commander is more difficult.

Cleopatra
Seondeok
Ishida Mitsunari
Pepin III

All of these commander’s heads are obtainable from strategic reserves. So DO NOT use gold heads on them.

## 🪙 𝐆𝐨𝐥𝐝:

> Seondeok is the best gold gathering commander overall, offering a 20% increased gathering speed + 30% increased gathering speed if you’re gathering gold, 10% extra resources once you finish gathering, and an extra 30% load.

## 🪨 𝐒𝐭𝐨𝐧𝐞:

> Cleopatra offers an extra 50% load, 20% increased gathering speed + 30% increased gathering speed if you’re gathering stone, and grey resource chests if you gather the whole node.

## 🪵 𝐖𝐨𝐨𝐝:

> Pepin offers an extra 50% load, 20% increased gathering speed + 30% increased gathering speed if you’re gathering wood, and 10% extra resources once you finish gathering.

## 🌽 𝐅𝐨𝐨𝐝:

> Ishida Mitsunari offers an extra 50% load, and 20% increased gathering speed for wood and stone + 30% increased gathering speed when gathering food.

Which commander you should prioritize depends on your personal playstyle and available resources, so decide which one to focus on first based on your situation.

⸻

## 📊 𝐆𝐚𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐁𝐨𝐧𝐮𝐬𝐞𝐬:

> It is important to note that there are two types of gathering bonuses provided by commanders:
• “Gathering Speed Bonus”
• “Bonus Gathering Speed (Specific Resource Type)”

These bonuses stack together, increasing your overall gathering efficiency.

You can view commander gathering bonuses in Screenshot 3.

## 🏗️ 𝐖𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐭𝐨 𝐅𝐨𝐜𝐮𝐬 𝐎𝐧 𝐖𝐡𝐞𝐧 𝐔𝐩𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚 𝐅𝐚𝐫𝐦:

> When developing a farm account, pay close attention to the following priorities:

## 1️⃣ 𝐓𝐨𝐰𝐧 𝐇𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐋𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐥:

> Your Town Hall level directly affects how many gathering marches you can send:
• Level 11 → 3 marches
• Level 17 → 4 marches
• Level 22 → 5 marches (maximum)

Reaching these milestones should be one of your main goals when upgrading a farm.

⸻

## 2️⃣ 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐄𝐪𝐮𝐢𝐩𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭:

> Focus primarily on upgrading gathering commanders only.

In addition, craft equipment that provides gathering bonuses, specifically:
• Reaper Set
• Forest Guardian Set

These sets significantly improve your resource collection efficiency.

⸻

## 3️⃣ 𝐓𝐫𝐚𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐨𝐬𝐭/𝐑𝐒𝐒 𝐓𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐟𝐞𝐫:

> Maximize the level of your Trading Post to greatly reduce transfer fees when sending resources to your main account.

Alternatively, there is a faster method to transfer resources:
• Withdraw all troops from your farm city.
• Plunder the city with your main account.

⚠️ Note: The downside of this method is that you will lose some troops when attacking the Watchtower.

⸻

## 4️⃣ 𝐕𝐈𝐏 𝐋𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐥:

> Upgrade your VIP level as soon as possible:
• VIP 6 → 10% gathering speed bonus
• VIP 11 → 20% gathering speed bonus

Higher VIP levels provide a significant long-term advantage for farming efficiency.

⸻

##📌 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐚𝐥 𝐓𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡𝐭𝐬:

> By now, you should understand the core concept of building a farm account.

The main goal is simple:

Make the account as gathering-oriented as possible.
